registerProjectWithWeb.description: Non-blocking HTTP call to register a project with the web service. Fails silently if service is unavailable.

isWebEnabled.description: Checks for RAPID_WEB=true in environment or .env file

register_web_skill.description: Skill definition for /rapid:register-web command to register legacy projects

install_web_extension.description: Additions to /rapid:install for optional web service setup

doctor_web_checks.description: Health checks for web service integrated into doctor command

health_endpoint.signature: GET /api/health -> {status: str, version: str, uptime: float}

register_project.signature: POST /api/projects {path: str, name: str} -> {id: str, status: str}

non_blocking.description: All HTTP calls to the web service must use fire-and-forget with a 2-second timeout; CLI must never hang waiting for web service

graceful_failure.description: If web service is unavailable, CLI logs a debug message and continues normally; no user-visible error

env_var_gating.description: All web service integration is gated by RAPID_WEB=true; without it, no HTTP calls are made

no_cli_modification.description: Existing CLI commands must behave identically with or without RAPID_WEB; web integration is purely additive

definition.scope: Integration between the existing RAPID Node.js CLI and the new Python web service. Modifications to /install for optional service setup, /init for auto-registration, new /register-web command for legacy projects, non-blocking HTTP helpers, and doctor checks.

tasks.description: Implement web-client.cjs with non-blocking HTTP helpers and RAPID_WEB detection
tasks.acceptance: HTTP calls timeout after 2 seconds, fail silently when service is down

tasks.description: Modify /rapid:install to optionally set up web service (systemd enable, env var)
tasks.acceptance: Install prompts for web service, writes RAPID_WEB=true to shell config

tasks.description: Modify /rapid:init to auto-register project when RAPID_WEB=true
tasks.acceptance: After init, project appears in web dashboard

tasks.description: Create /rapid:register-web skill for legacy project registration
tasks.acceptance: Legacy projects can be registered with a single command

tasks.description: Add web service health checks to doctor command
tasks.acceptance: Doctor reports service status, DB accessibility, port availability

acceptance: CLI works identically with RAPID_WEB unset
All HTTP calls are non-blocking with 2-second timeout
Doctor command reports web service health
